mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for FEDFUNDS levels exceeds +0.05, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rate increase periods.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.5pp, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.02 during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand strength that boosts corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, with interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects immediate supply-side contraction that triggers short-term equity sell-offs, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances during tightening cycles, or the moving average crossover signal shows zero predictive accuracy (AUC < 0.52).

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with low policy rates creates a negative real interest rate environment that fuels equity risk premiums and speculative positioning, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces negative 60-day returns in >60% of triggered cases, or the real rate differential loses explanatory power (adjusted R-squared < 0.05) in quarterly return models.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the consumer base and supports corporate earnings growth, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS levels falls below 0.01, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical difference from zero during periods of positive payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP growth below 0.5% indicates insufficient aggregate demand to sustain corporate profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns exceed +2% in >50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 0.5%, or the threshold signal fails to separate bull and bear markets (Sharpe ratio difference < 0.3).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling housing starts signals inventory buildup and weakening downstream demand, triggering immediate short-term equity corrections over the next week.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 5-day returns in >65% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from noise (p > 0.15) in high-frequency return regressions.
